text: Diego wants pi-intercom published to npm so The Last Harness can install it via npm instead of checking out this repository during installer runs; the motivation is faster TLH installation.

The release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) publishes with 'npm publish --provenance' and relies on npm OIDC Trusted Publishing, which the maintainer confirmed is already enabled for @diegopetrucci/pi-intercom on npmjs.com. There is intentionally no NPM_TOKEN secret. If publishing ever fails on auth, check the Trusted Publisher config on the npm package settings rather than adding a token. CI/release is a deliberate fork-only delta: upstream nicobailon/pi-intercom has no GitHub Actions at all.

On 2026-07-14 Diego chose a non-destructive TLH surface trim for pi-intercom: keep full as the compatibility default; add bridge mode that unregisters the generic intercom/contact_supervisor tools, /intercom, and Alt+M while preserving rich pi-subagents result/control relay, wake-up, acknowledgements, and broker compatibility; add off as the extension hard stop. Keep implementation and skill source intact for upstream intake. TLH should enable bridge only after deploying and validating pi-subagents native supervision; rich-result/control delivery is the temporary remaining bridge responsibility.

On 2026-07-21 Diego authorized a separate draft-only pi-intercom PR to retire subagent control-relay registration from bridge mode while preserving full-mode compatibility and the bridge result relay/delivery acknowledgements. Do not merge this change until native control/attention and wait wake-up parity in diegopetrucci/pi-subagents#66 is merged and validated.
author: Diego Petrucci <baulei@icloud.com>
